corpus_and_ground_truth:
INVERTED GROUND-TRUTH / RETRIEVAL-FIRST FLYWHEEL — how it works in code:

The "ground truth" is not a labeled training set shipped with the repo; it is an empty-at-cold-start, self-hosted corpus of (FaultSignature, Plan, OutcomeLabel) triples that the engine *generates as it operates* and *gates on the way in*. "Inverted" = the corpus and weights live in a SEPARATE private repo; this repo ships only the client + schema and actively blocks corpus data from entering the tree (README.md:18-26; .gitignore /corpus/ /weights/ *.gguf *.safetensors; scripts/githooks/pre-commit grep guard).

The flywheel loop, in code:
1. RETRIEVAL: support-agent/src/main.rs:289 `corpus.query(&signature, &config_class)` looks up confirmed precedents, scoped to the SAME config class (corpus-client/src/store.rs:26-53 `fix_mappings` filters on fingerprint == AND config_class == AND label.is_resolved()). Only RESOLVED rows back a FixMapping; failures stay as hard negatives but are never offered (store.rs:35-38, tested store.rs:334-348).
2. RETRIEVAL-FIRST: main.rs:304-324 turns precedents into `CandidateSource::CorpusPrimed` candidates; main.rs:318 `retrieval_first` flag; main.rs:332 skips de-novo model generation when precedent exists.
3. PRIOR: panel/src/lib.rs:248-254 — CorpusPrimed scores likelihood 0.8 vs 0.6 for a cold guess of the same shape, so precedent wins best_of_n (tested panel/src/lib.rs:360-368).
4. WRITE-BACK: main.rs:690-711 `record_outcome` -> `Contribution::new` (de-identify) -> `corpus.submit` (sign-off gate) -> append. Across runs via FileCorpus (store.rs:128-198, one JSON row/line), so run N+1 facing the same signature starts from run N's outcome (tested main.rs:1037-1071 `the_flywheel_turns_a_resolved_outcome_into_a_precedent`).

FaultSignature (common/src/fault.rs) = order-independent FNV fingerprint over structured symptoms; ConfigClass (common/src/config_class.rs) = BOM revision OR derived inventory hash. Both carry no identity (common/src/hash.rs). The corpus row is de-identified BY CONSTRUCTION: it is built only from extract_symptoms output + action-vocabulary plans, so PII never had a path in (corpus-client/src/lib.rs:34-127 adversarial leakage suite, SEEDED_IDENTIFIERS at lib.rs:43-51).
summary:
cec-support-agent is a Rust Cargo workspace (10 crates) implementing an open diagnostic engine: a support request flows through intake interview → diagnostics/signature → routing → candidate generation (swarm) → judge panel → sign-off-gated execution → verification → labeled corpus write-back. The "inverted ground truth" design: the corpus (fault-signature→fix mappings + outcomes) and model weights live in a SEPARATE private repo and are never present here; this repo ships only the corpus CLIENT + SCHEMA, with the pre-commit hook and .gitignore blocking corpus/weight files. Ground truth is built up at runtime by the flywheel: confirmed outcomes are written back (gate-checked + de-identified) and served retrieval-first to later runs of the same signature at the same config class.

Evidence integrity is enforced at FOUR existing chokepoints, all in code (not docs): (1) de-identification by structured EXTRACTION not scrubbing — common/src/extract.rs builds a content-free FaultSignature from a fixed vocabulary, and corpus-client de_identify_plan strips plans to action vocabulary, with an adversarial leakage test suite; (2) the sign-off GATE — corpus_client::ensure_signed_off refuses any contribution not verifier/human-confirmed, called in every submit path before any disk/network write; (3) plan PROVENANCE — HMAC-SHA256 judge signing (provenance crate) re-verified at the executor (execute_signed_plan) so only the judge's exact plan crosses into the on-machine zone; (4) the CONSENT gate — the dispatcher refuses any tool whose risk exceeds granted consent. Verification (agent-core/src/verify.rs) diffs a re-collected signature against the original to decide pass/provisional/fail.

The main GAP for an "evidence integrity / research checklist": there is NO single enforced checkpoint that binds verification evidence, provenance signature, de-identification proof, and config-class provenance together before write-back. record_outcome/Contribution::new trust the caller's SignOff value and label; the verification verdict is computed but not cryptographically bound to the contribution; and the bootstrap collector re-derives the post-signature from the SAME request text (main.rs:556-559) so it structurally cannot observe a real fix yet. These are the natural hook points for a checklist.

crates.name: common
crates.role: Shared domain vocabulary + the de-identification primitive. Defines Plan/PlanStep/Risk, Candidate/CandidateSource, FaultSignature/Symptom, ConfigClass, DiagnosticEvent, ExecutionResult, Fluency, and the FNV hash. Owns structured symptom extraction (extract_symptoms) — the load-bearing de-identification mechanism.
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: DE-IDENTIFICATION BY CONSTRUCTION. extract.rs:73 extract_symptoms keeps ONLY a fixed fault VOCABULARY (extract.rs:15-54), 0x-hex codes (extract.rs:125), prefix-bound numeric ids (extract.rs:107-113, ID_PREFIXES extract.rs:60-62), ALL-CAPS stop-code names (extract.rs:132-139), and bare module names (extract.rs:143-153); hostnames/usernames/paths/serials are DROPPED because they match no rule (tested drops_identity_bearing_text extract.rs:210-225). fault.rs:39-45 recurring_in is the verification diff primitive. hash.rs fingerprint is order-independent and carries no identity. This is the de-identification half of evidence integrity — the corpus row is content-free before it exists.

crates.name: intake
crates.role: Stage 0 — the intake judge. Maps a free-text request to a structured Case via the CompTIA-step-1 helpdesk interview. Deterministic, model-free FUNNEL (which field, what order, when to stop); model only PHRASES questions behind the Interviewer trait with scripted fallback. Assesses Fluency register from how reasoned the reporter's own words are (quoted codes capped at 1 point).
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: Evidence-vs-fluency separation (intake/src/lib.rs:592-643 assess_fluency: RECITED_TERMS lib.rs:579-590 are evidence about the MACHINE, capped, never read as reporter competence). Case.brief() (lib.rs:149-154) is built ONLY from enum fields so it is prompt-safe (tested the_brief_is_built_only_from_enums lib.rs:1195). Every answer runs through extract_symptoms (lib.rs:306) so a code typed at the prompt lands in the signature; statement/transcript are ticket context and never enter the corpus (lib.rs:36-38, 338-342). reproducibility (lib.rs:89-101) sets the later verification class. NOTE: register/fluency calibrates teaching only, never safety or what is checked (fluency.rs:5-9).

crates.name: panel
crates.role: Stages 3 & 7 — routing taxonomy + judge panel. route_for classifies software-state / hardware-evidenced / ambiguous BEFORE scoring; HeuristicJudge scores 4 axes; best_of_n picks the winner; required_escalation sets the sign-off bar (Auto/VerifierConfirm/HumanConfirm).
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: ESCALATION ENFORCEMENT. required_escalation (panel/src/lib.rs:317-331): a hardware-evidenced or ambiguous route ALWAYS raises to HumanConfirm (lib.rs:324-326); any state-changing plan with NO sandbox validation raises to HumanConfirm — 'unvalidated equals escalate' (lib.rs:327-329); escalation is independent of judge confidence (a high score never lowers it). escalation_for (lib.rs:299-306) forces HumanConfirm for destructive plans. Corpus prior lives here too (lib.rs:248-254): CorpusPrimed likelihood 0.8 vs 0.6, the retrieval-first preference. Route.explanation (lib.rs:44-101) is plain-language by contract.

crates.name: swarm
crates.role: Stage 5 (generation fan-out) + stage 6 coordination surface. Swarm.gather fans diagnostics out to a slate of Generators with GRACEFUL degradation (a failed generator is recorded, not fatal); defines SandboxValidator/ValidationReport traits for sandbox-VM validation (no backend wired in the CLI).
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: Graceful degradation (swarm/src/lib.rs:101-118): one dead generator/endpoint never empties the slate; only an empty generator SET is an error; failures recorded in Gathered.failures (lib.rs:122-128). ValidationReport (lib.rs:52-60) is the sandbox-evidence record that required_escalation consumes — but the CLI configures NO validator (main.rs:376 sandbox_validated=false), so sandbox evidence is currently always absent (a gap: the validation-evidence integrity stage is a trait with no production implementation here).

crates.name: agent-core
crates.role: Stages 8-10 — Tool trait, consent-gated Dispatcher, execution loop, signed-plan executor, and the verify_outcome stage. The on-machine enforcement boundary.
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: THREE enforcement points. (1) PROVENANCE re-verify: execute.rs:20 key.verify(signed)? refuses a tampered/unsigned plan BEFORE any tool runs (tested lib.rs:242-262). (2) CONSENT gate: dispatch.rs:70 `if !consent.permits(risk)` refuses any over-risk tool at dispatch, not call site (consent.rs:21-29; tested lib.rs:110-124). (3) VERIFICATION: verify.rs:48-65 verify_outcome diffs re-collected vs original signature -> Pass/ProvisionalPass/Fail{recurring}/OffMachine; hardware is OffMachine even when clean (verify.rs:53); intermittent only PAROLES (verify.rs:62). dispatch.rs:32 contains() is the operation-vocabulary check (advisory-only enforcement). NOTE: execution stops at first failed/refused step (execute.rs:51-53) so a remediation never keeps applying after error.

crates.name: provenance
crates.role: Plan signing — the agent-neutrality boundary. The judge holds a SigningKey, signs the winning Plan with HMAC-SHA256 over its canonical JSON; the executor verifies before any step runs.
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: SIGNING/INTEGRITY mechanism. SigningKey.sign (provenance/src/lib.rs:63-70) binds signature to canonical(plan) (lib.rs:89-91, serde_json bytes); verify (lib.rs:74-80) is constant-time via HMAC verify_slice; any step/action/risk tamper breaks it (tested lib.rs:130-139). Key is symmetric in-process; key custody, rotation, signature-format evolution, and AUDIT-LOG RETENTION are explicitly LEFT OPEN as deployment policy (lib.rs:11-16) — a documented gap a checklist could close. Canonicalization relies on struct field order (lib.rs:88), not a sorted/canonical-JSON encoder — a subtle integrity caveat.

crates.name: corpus-client
crates.role: The only contact point with the (private) corpus. Owns the sign-off GATE, the de-identification of plans at contribution build, the six-value OutcomeLabel schema, config-class-scoped retrieval, the file-backed flywheel store, and the adversarial leakage test suite.
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: THE corpus-write integrity surface. gate.rs:15 ensure_signed_off refuses any contribution whose SignOff is not Verifier/HumanConfirmed; called BEFORE any state change in LocalCorpus (store.rs:116), FileCorpus (store.rs:184, before disk touch — tested store.rs:393-409), and HttpCorpus (store.rs:251, before network). schema.rs:107-114 Contribution::new de_identifies the plan so a raw plan cannot enter a contribution; de_identify_plan (schema.rs:122-141) keeps only id + ordered actions + per-step risk, dropping titles/descriptions. lib.rs:34-127 leakage suite seeds 7 identifiers (lib.rs:43-51) through the whole path and asserts zero leakage in the serialized row. Only is_resolved() rows back FixMappings (schema.rs:51-61); hard negatives kept but never retrieved (store.rs:35-38).

crates.name: tools-windows
crates.role: The concrete Windows tools (operation vocabulary): cim_query, event_log_query, create_restore_point, registry_set, board_info, download_file. Each declares a Risk so the consent gate applies. Stub 'unsupported' off-Windows. Also firmware advisories.
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: Per-tool integrity safeguards. board_info uses an explicit Select-Object ALLOWLIST so SerialNumber/asset tags are never even queried (lib.rs:301-342, configuration fields only). create_restore_point POSITIVELY VERIFIES a checkpoint was actually created (reads it back, catches the silent 24h skip; lib.rs:245-295) and states its coverage boundary (no BIOS/firmware/user files). registry_set captures a backup before any write and aborts if backup fails (lib.rs:194-205). download_file is HTTPS-ONLY + injection-safe URL/filename validation, confined to Downloads\cec-support, reports SHA-256, never installs (lib.rs:348-427, validated_url/validated_file_name). safe_identifier (lib.rs:60-69) blocks PowerShell injection. firmware flashing is ADVISORY-ONLY by design (advisory.rs) — never agent-executed; advisory carries no identity fields (tested advisory.rs:387-397). Off-Windows -> 'unsupported' so a non-Windows run halts and labels unresolved rather than claiming a fix.

crates.name: inference
crates.role: OpenAI-compatible HTTP client with a pluggable endpoint and configurable timeouts. No hardwired provider/model/GPU. Cold-starts against localhost with no outbound call.
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: Availability-integrity (degradation). client.rs:11 DEFAULT_TIMEOUT 120s and Endpoint.with_timeout (client.rs:46-49) ensure a saturated endpoint FAILS instead of hanging, so every model call is on a degradable path (client.rs:24-26). Empty choices -> InferenceError::EmptyResponse (client.rs:115-117). Not directly an evidence-integrity surface, but the boundary that keeps a dead/hostile endpoint from stalling the gated pipeline. Not yet enforced: no response provenance, no TLS-pinning, no max-tokens/content auditing on model output before it becomes a plan step.

crates.name: support-agent
crates.role: The headless CLI that WIRES the full eight-stage pipeline: intake -> diagnostics/signature -> config class -> route -> board enrichment -> corpus query -> retrieval-first generation/swarm -> sandbox(none) -> judge -> sign-off gate -> sign+execute -> verify -> label -> corpus write-back, with model tiering and bounded retry.
crates.evidence_provenance_relevance: Where every gate is composed and where a checklist would attach. Sign-off-vs-escalation check main.rs:428-434 (verifier refused when judge requires human). Consent grant derived from sign-off main.rs:440-444. Executability/operation-vocabulary filter main.rs:451-456 + is_executable main.rs:615-621. Judge signs then execute_signed_plan re-verifies main.rs:526-531. Verification main.rs:558-561 (CAVEAT: post-signature re-derived from the SAME request text main.rs:556-559, so the bootstrap collector structurally CANNOT observe a real fix — an evidence-integrity gap). label_for main.rs:775-792, record_outcome -> Contribution::new -> gate main.rs:690-711. Rendered consent main.rs:627-650 with restore-point coverage boundary; decline -> Withdrawn main.rs:507-521. Bounded retry MAX_ATTEMPTS=2 main.rs:609, failed attempt recorded as hard negative then escalate.

pipeline_stages: Stage 0 — INTAKE (owner: intake crate; wired main.rs:164-209). Interview::new infers case fields from the description, asks helpdesk follow-ups only for open fields (deterministic funnel, model only phrases). Produces a Case + de-identified signature + Fluency register. CompTIA step 1.

Stage 1 — DIAGNOSTICS COLLECTION + SIGNATURE (owner: support-agent collect_diagnostics main.rs:713-723 + common::extract_symptoms; signature_of main.rs:728-736). Builds the de-identified FaultSignature. Bootstrap seeds one event from request text; a real run gathers logs/WER/WHEA/CIM via tools-windows.
Stage 2 — CONFIG CLASS (owner: common::ConfigClass; host_config_class main.rs:742-747). Scopes every corpus row/query to like configs: BOM revision or derived inventory hash.

Stage 3 — ROUTING (owner: panel::route_for; main.rs:232-275). Software-state / hardware-evidenced{part_class} / ambiguous, decided BEFORE scoring; hardware evidence outranks software. Triggers board_info enrichment + firmware advisory when platform/driver implicated.

Stage 4/5 — CANDIDATE GENERATION, retrieval-first (owner: corpus-client query + swarm::Swarm.gather + support-agent generators; main.rs:289-371). Confirmed precedents join as CorpusPrimed and skip de-novo generation; else hypothesis-seeded ModelGenerators (HYPOTHESES main.rs:804-818) + always-present HeuristicGenerator fan out; failures degrade gracefully.

Stage 6 — SANDBOX VALIDATION (owner: swarm::SandboxValidator trait; main.rs:376-377). Trait defined; CLI wires NO backend, so sandbox_validated=false and 'unvalidated equals escalate' fires in the panel.

Stage 7 — JUDGE PANEL (owner: panel::best_of_n + required_escalation; main.rs:381-414). Scores the slate, picks best-of-N, sets the escalation/sign-off bar from route + validation state + risk/score ladder.

Stage 8 — SIGN-OFF-GATED EXECUTION + VERIFY + LABEL + WRITE-BACK (owner: provenance + agent-core + corpus-client; main.rs:417-603). Sign-off must meet required escalation (main.rs:428-434); consent derived from sign-off; judge signs plan; execute_signed_plan re-verifies signature; verify_outcome diffs re-collected vs original signature; label_for emits one of six OutcomeLabels; record_outcome de-identifies + submits through ensure_signed_off; bounded retry then escalate.
evidence_integrity_points: DE-IDENTIFICATION BY EXTRACTION (signature side): common/src/extract.rs:73 extract_symptoms — fixed vocabulary (extract.rs:15-54), 0x-hex (extract.rs:125-127), prefix-bound ids (extract.rs:107-113), ALL-CAPS stop codes (extract.rs:132-139), module names (extract.rs:143-153). Identity dropped because it matches no rule (test extract.rs:210-225). fault.rs FaultSignature carries no identity by contract (fault.rs:13-22).

DE-IDENTIFICATION BY EXTRACTION (plan side): corpus-client/src/schema.rs:107-114 Contribution::new always calls de_identify_plan; de_identify_plan (schema.rs:122-141) keeps only id + ordered action vocabulary + per-step risk, dropping title and step descriptions where prose/PII hides.

ADVERSARIAL LEAKAGE PROOF: corpus-client/src/lib.rs:34-127 — seeds 7 known identifiers (lib.rs:43-51) through a hostile request+plan and asserts zero leakage into the serialized row (lib.rs:79-90) while structured evidence survives (lib.rs:92-106).

SIGN-OFF GATE: corpus-client/src/gate.rs:15-21 ensure_signed_off — Ok only if SignOff is VerifierConfirmed|HumanConfirmed (schema.rs:17-22). Enforced before ANY state change in all three stores: store.rs:116 (Local), store.rs:184 (File, before disk — test store.rs:393-409), store.rs:251 (Http, before network).

PLAN PROVENANCE / SIGNING: provenance/src/lib.rs:63-80 HMAC-SHA256 sign/verify over canonical(plan); re-verified at the executor in agent-core/src/execute.rs:20 key.verify(signed)? BEFORE any tool runs (test agent-core/src/lib.rs:242-262). Wired at main.rs:526-531.

CONSENT GATE: agent-core/src/dispatch.rs:59-74 — dispatch refuses any tool whose risk exceeds granted Consent (dispatch.rs:70). consent.rs:21-29 least-privilege ladder. Execution halts at first refused/failed step (execute.rs:51-53).

OPERATION-VOCABULARY (advisory-only) CHECK: dispatch.rs:32 Dispatcher.contains; main.rs:615-621 is_executable filters plans to registered tools; out-of-vocabulary plans (e.g. model 'review' steps, firmware flashing) are advisory-only and never executed (main.rs:451-480).

OUTCOME VERIFICATION: agent-core/src/verify.rs:48-65 verify_outcome diffs re-collected signature against the original; intermittent only paroles (ProvisionalPass), hardware is OffMachine even when clean (verify.rs:53-63). Class chosen from route + reproducibility (main.rs:763-771).

ESCALATION ENFORCEMENT: panel/src/lib.rs:317-331 required_escalation — hardware/ambiguous route forces HumanConfirm; unvalidated state-changing plan forces HumanConfirm; independent of confidence. CLI refuses a verifier sign-off when human is required (main.rs:428-434).

POSITIVELY-VERIFIED SAFETY EVIDENCE in tools: create_restore_point reads the checkpoint back and matches description, catching the silent 24h skip (tools-windows/src/lib.rs:245-295); registry_set backs up before write and aborts on backup failure (lib.rs:194-205); board_info Select-Object allowlist excludes serials (lib.rs:321-329); download_file is HTTPS-only + injection-validated + SHA-256-reported, never installs (lib.rs:348-427).

LABELING DISCIPLINE: every ticket emits an OutcomeLabel including failures/withdrawals (schema.rs:24-49; label_for main.rs:775-792), because an unlabeled ticket is 'corpus poison' — failures stored as hard negatives, never offered as fixes (store.rs:35-38, test store.rs:334-348).

REPO-LEVEL INTEGRITY: pre-commit hook (scripts/githooks/pre-commit) blocks corpus/weights dirs + model/db formats and runs gitleaks protect + fmt; .gitignore mirrors it; CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) runs fmt/clippy -D warnings/build/test on 3 OSes + cargo deny + gitleaks. SECURITY.md:scope names sign-off bypass, consent bypass, corpus/weight leak, and hidden network calls as security issues.
where_checklist_would_hook: PRIMARY HOOK — before corpus write-back, inside corpus-client/src/schema.rs Contribution::new (schema.rs:107) OR a new ensure_evidence_integrity() alongside gate.rs:ensure_signed_off called from every CorpusStore::submit (store.rs:116/184/251). This is the one funnel every row passes through; a checklist enforced here cannot be bypassed. Today the gate only checks SignOff.is_confirmed() — extend it to also require: a bound verification verdict, a non-empty structured signature, a present config class, and a provenance/signature attestation.

AT THE SIGN-OFF GATE itself (corpus-client/src/gate.rs:15) — turn ensure_signed_off into a checklist evaluator that returns a structured GateError per failed item, and have it consume an evidence bundle rather than just contribution.sign_off (which is currently a caller-supplied enum with no proof behind it).

AT VERIFICATION (support-agent/src/main.rs:558-573, agent-core/src/verify.rs) — bind the Verdict to the Contribution. Currently verify_outcome's result feeds label_for but is NOT carried into the corpus row; a checklist item 'resolved labels must be backed by a Pass/ProvisionalPass verdict over the same instrument' would hook here. Also flag the bootstrap caveat that post-signature is re-derived from the same request text (main.rs:556-559).

AT PROVENANCE SIGNING (provenance/src/lib.rs:63 sign / agent-core/src/execute.rs:20 verify, wired main.rs:526-531) — extend SignedPlan to carry/attest the judge identity, route, escalation, and config class, so the corpus row can prove the plan it stores is the exact plan the judge signed AND the run that produced it. The crate doc (lib.rs:11-16) already flags key custody/rotation/audit-log as open — a checklist item.

AT THE EXECUTABILITY / OPERATION-VOCABULARY check (main.rs:451-480, is_executable main.rs:615) — checklist item: only in-vocabulary, signed, consented plans may be recorded as having executed; advisory-only plans must be labeled as advice, not fixes.

AT RECORD_OUTCOME (support-agent/src/main.rs:690-711) — the call-site wrapper. A pre-submit checklist run here (before Contribution::new) gives the CLI a place to refuse/annotate, but the authoritative enforcement must still live in corpus-client so library embedders (MyOwnLLM, AllMyStuff) cannot skip it.

AS A REPO/CI GATE — mirror the runtime checklist as a test/CI invariant (extend the leakage suite at corpus-client/src/lib.rs:34 and the SECURITY.md invariant list) so 'a checklist item with no adversarial test is a checklist item that silently regresses' — matching the existing pattern that an untested scrub pass leaks.
gaps: NO unified evidence-integrity checkpoint exists. The sign-off gate (gate.rs:15) checks ONLY contribution.sign_off.is_confirmed(); it does not verify that the contribution actually carries a verification verdict, a provenance attestation, or a de-identification proof. The four integrity mechanisms (extraction, gate, signing, consent) are enforced at four DIFFERENT places and never jointly bound to the row being written.

SignOff is caller-asserted, not proven. Contribution.sign_off (schema.rs:100) is a plain enum the CLI sets from --sign-off (main.rs:115-123, 698-706). Nothing cryptographically ties it to an actual human/verifier action — a caller can construct Contribution{ sign_off: HumanConfirmed } directly and the gate passes. There is no signature over the (signature, plan, label, sign_off) tuple.

Verification verdict is NOT carried into the corpus row. verify_outcome's Verdict (verify.rs) is computed (main.rs:560) and used to pick a label (label_for main.rs:775), but the row stores only the OutcomeLabel, not the recurring-symptom diff or the verification class — so a 'resolved' row cannot later be audited against the evidence that justified it.

Bootstrap verification cannot observe a real fix. The post-execution signature is re-derived from the SAME request text (main.rs:556-559 collect_diagnostics(&args.describe)), so in the CLI bootstrap the diff is always empty and any completed software-state run trivially labels ResolvedConfirmed. The 'same instrument' guarantee (verify.rs:46) is only as good as a real re-collection, which is not wired.

Provenance signing key is in-process and ephemeral. main.rs:485 SigningKey::generate() mints a fresh key per run; there is no judge-identity binding, no key custody/rotation, and no audit-log retention (explicitly deferred at provenance/src/lib.rs:11-16). The signature proves intra-run integrity only, not WHICH judge signed.

Plan canonicalization is field-order JSON, not a canonical encoder. provenance canonical() (lib.rs:88-91) relies on serde struct field order; it is not a sorted/canonical-JSON form, so signature stability is coupled to struct definitions — a fragile integrity assumption for cross-version or cross-language verification.

Sandbox-validation evidence is structurally absent. The SandboxValidator trait (swarm/src/lib.rs:64-68) has no production implementation wired in the CLI (main.rs:376 hardcodes false). 'Unvalidated equals escalate' covers the safety case, but there is no actual sandbox-evidence integrity stage — validation reports are never produced or checked.

Model output integrity is unchecked. ModelGenerator (main.rs:846-893) puts raw model content directly into a PlanStep.description with action 'review' and ReadOnly risk; there is no validation, claimed-risk vs actual-action reconciliation, or de-identification of model output before it becomes a candidate (it is only de-identified later if it reaches a contribution).

No inference-channel provenance. inference/src/client.rs does TLS via rustls but no cert pinning, no response attestation, and no audit of which endpoint/model produced a candidate — a compromised or swapped endpoint is invisible to the corpus row.

FileCorpus has no integrity/tamper protection. Rows are plain appended JSONL (store.rs:181-197) with no per-row signature, hash chain, or append-only enforcement beyond OS file perms; an operator can hand-edit confirmed precedents that later get served retrieval-first as authoritative fixes.

Config-class provenance is coarse and unverified in bootstrap. host_config_class (main.rs:742-747) hashes only OS+ARCH, not the real CIM hardware/driver inventory the design intends, so retrieval scoping is weaker than documented; nothing attests that the config class on a row matches the machine it came from.
